journalQuestions: SPC’s FHRI_max is 58.6 — close to the Functional / Mission-Aligned threshold. From your experience as a student, which parameter feels most present as a structural constraint: ε, φ, or μ? What would a small improvement look like in practice?

The exit-option modulator says students can leave extractive higher-education institutions, although often at high cost. From the perspective of a student who has already borrowed money, how free is the exit option actually?

Miami Dade and SPC have similar public missions but an 8.1-point FHRI_max gap in the source app. What data would you request first if you were asked to investigate this gap as a governance consultant?

The source app treats the 1992 HEA amendments as a P-adic access mechanism for higher education Interpretant replacement. What would a T1 structural intervention change if it addressed the access condition rather than only regulating downstream symptoms?
Looking toward your future sector, what institutional signals would suggest that an employer or professional setting is shifting from mission to extraction?

evidenceStatus.Public data sources named in source app: College Scorecard API, IPEDS Finance survey, prior IPS audits for cross-sector benchmarks.
evidenceStatus.Date / run note from source app: Higher-education values described as live April 2026 API runs; IPEDS Finance values described as embedded 2022–23 where used.
evidenceStatus.Directly measured or source-reported: Institutional spending categories, median debt, median earnings, repayment, completion and enrollment-related fields, where available.
evidenceStatus.Computed model output: FHRI_max and IPS parameters ε, φ, ρ, σ, μ.
evidenceStatus.Interpretive model claim: Mission alignment, extraction floor, exit-option modulator, and Interpretant replacement.
evidenceStatus.Verification needed: Replicate exact API pulls, formula implementation, missing-data handling, and cross-sector benchmark derivation before using as reviewer-facing empirical proof.
